meta.title: iSkitch — Capture, annotate & share for macOS
meta.description: iSkitch is a modern, native macOS take on the classic Skitch — capture any region, window or full screen, annotate it instantly, and share with one click.

blog.index_description: Practical guides to capturing, annotating, hiding sensitive info and sharing screenshots on macOS — from the makers of iSkitch.

features.title: Built for the macOS you use today
features.lead: Native SwiftUI + AppKit. Lives in the menu bar. Designed to get out of your way until you press ⌥⌘4.

items.title: Capture anything
items.desc: Region (⌥⌘4), window or full screen. Opens straight in the editor.

items.title: 14 annotation tools
items.desc: Tapered arrows, text with system fonts, shapes, highlighter, pen, stamps and more.

items.title: Hide sensitive info
items.desc: Pixelate, blur, solid block or stripes — reliably unreadable.

items.title: Native sharing
items.desc: macOS share sheet, drag-out to any app, copy or save as PNG/JPG/PDF.

items.title: Spotlight & crop
items.desc: Focus attention on what matters. Crop with aspect ratio presets.

items.title: Privacy first
items.desc: No accounts. No tracking. No data leaves your Mac.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.lead: Everything you need to know about iSkitch — capture, pricing, privacy and more.

items.a: iSkitch is a modern, native macOS app to capture your screen, annotate it instantly and share it in one click. It’s an independent, ground-up recreation of the classic Skitch experience, rebuilt in SwiftUI and AppKit for today’s macOS. It is not made by or affiliated with Evernote or the original Skitch.
iSkitch is a one-time purchase of US$9.99 (or the equivalent in your local currency) — no subscription, ever. Buy it once and it’s yours, including future updates.

items.q: Which Macs and macOS versions does it support?
items.a: macOS 14 (Sonoma) or later. iSkitch is a Universal Binary, so it runs natively on both Apple Silicon (M-series) and Intel Macs.
Eight: English, Spanish, French, German, Italian, Portuguese, Japanese and Korean. It follows your system language automatically, and you can change it in Settings ▸ General ▸ Language.

items.q: I press ⌥⌘4 but nothing is captured. What’s wrong?
items.a: It’s almost always the Screen Recording permission. Open System Settings ▸ Privacy & Security ▸ Screen Recording, turn on iSkitch, and relaunch the app. The first time you capture, macOS asks for this permission automatically.

Nowhere until you decide. iSkitch never saves silently: you choose the destination when you press Save or drag the image out of the window. In Settings ▸ Capture you can set a default folder and format (PNG, JPG, PDF or TIFF).

items.q: How do I share or export a capture?
items.a: Click Share to use the native macOS share sheet (Mail, Messages, Notes, Slack, AirDrop and more), copy to the clipboard, drag the image straight into any app, or export to PNG, JPEG, PDF or TIFF.
Yes. Use the Obscure tool and choose Pixelate, Blur, Solid block or Stripes to reliably cover anything you don’t want to show.
No. iSkitch works entirely offline — no accounts, no tracking, no analytics — and your captures never leave your Mac.

footer.made: Made with ❤ from Madrid and Herencia (Ciudad Real) — Spain.

changelog.title: What’s new in iSkitch
changelog.lead: Every iSkitch release, newest first. Updates are free for everyone who owns the app.

items.text: Accessibility: VoiceOver now names the controls, tools, colors and annotations with their state, and you can work without a mouse — arrow keys move the selection and define a capture, Return confirms.
Region and window capture works on any display, not just the main one.
Copy the image from the Edit menu with a shortcut (⌘⇧C), and Undo/Redo are in the menu too.
Copying is now instant, and capturing straight to the clipboard confirms when it is done.
Cropping responds to the keyboard (Escape cancels, Return applies) and behaves the same from every menu.
Fixed a memory leak in long sessions, undo after cropping or rotating, and a save that could fail silently.
Saving or copying a large screenshot no longer loses your annotations: the file was written without them and the window closed as if saved. Now it warns you and exports nothing.
Hidden areas are always fully opaque: pixelate and blur no longer fall back to a translucent gray that could still be read.
Saving and sharing full-screen captures is much faster — they were processed at four times the size needed.
The default save folder is remembered between launches, and watching the system screenshots folder no longer stops when you change it.
iSkitch no longer even requests network access. It never used it: no telemetry, no update checks, no connections.
Edit a text annotation after creating it: double-click it with the Select tool, or click it with the Text tool to change the words.
Selecting a text now shows its color, font, size and bold/italic in the top bar, so you can restyle it without switching tools.
New text now keeps the font you’ve chosen from the very first character.
Precision tool cursors: every drawing tool (and crop) shows a crosshair with a mini icon of the active tool, switching instantly when you change tools.
The editor window now opens sized to your capture at 100% zoom when it fits on screen — no more fixed window with a shrunken image.
On multiple displays, the editor opens on the screen where you’re working, not always the main one.
Numbered stamps (1·2·3) that auto-increment — perfect for tutorials and runbooks.
Capture timer: a 3/5/10s countdown before the shot, to open a menu or trigger a hover state.
Recapture the last region with a single shortcut, without selecting again.
Multi-display: choose which screen to capture in full screen.
Quick capture: send a region or the full screen straight to the clipboard or a file, skipping the editor.
Copy to clipboard at full size (1x) so Retina screenshots paste at full resolution — plus a size and resolution (DPI) readout in the editor.
Cropping now shows your annotations too, so you crop the full composition.
Crop now fits the whole image to the window, with everything selected by default.
Crop handles and the rule-of-thirds grid stay a comfortable size at any zoom.
Fixed a stray resize cursor when opening an image.
The full macOS menu bar (File, Edit, Capture, Image, Tools) now reliably appears in the editor.
Clearer Save button — pick the format from a dedicated menu and save in one click.
Expanded, fully localized About window.
Open the editor automatically after a system screenshot.
Choose what happens after each capture: show, copy or save.
Customisable capture shortcut.
Added French, German, Italian, Portuguese, Japanese and Korean.
Expanded, fully localized About window.
Many editor refinements.
First release: capture a region, window or the full screen.
14 annotation tools and native macOS sharing.
Privacy-first: no accounts, no tracking, nothing leaves your Mac.
